[Learn more](https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/86640)\nSometimes you may be asked to solve the CAPTCHA if you are using advanced terms that robots are known to use, or sending requests very quickly. \n IP address: 2600:1900:0:4006::c01\nTime: 2026-04-14T07:19:21Z\nURL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SlHcblGFwfg", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "Forbes Most Valuable NBATeams2025 List: All 30 Franchise Values", "url": "https://www.forbes.com/sites/justinteitelbaum/2025/10/23/the-most-valuable-nba-teams-2025/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: Forbes Most Valuable NBA Teams 2025 List: All 30 Franchise Values URL Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/justinteitelbaum/2025/10/23/the-most-valuable-nba-teams-2025/ Markdown Content: ## Pro basketball\u2019s 30 franchises are all soaring high above the rim\u2014worth $5.4 billion on average, thanks to record sales of the Lakers and Celtics\u2014but no one is scoring quite like one $11 billion team. P**rivate equity billionaire Mark Walter\u2019s**[agreement in June](https://www.forbes.com/sites/antoniopequenoiv/2025/06/18/buss-family-will-sell-lakers-to-mark-walter-at-10-billion-valuation-the-most-expensive-sale-in-sports-history/ \"https://www.forbes.com/sites/antoniopequenoiv/2025/06/18/buss-family-will-sell-lakers-to-mark-walter-at-10-billion-valuation-the-most-expensive-sale-in-sports-history/\") to buy the Los Angeles Lakers at a $10 billion valuation, currently awaiting approval by the NBA, is set to establish a record sale price for a control stake in any sports team. Yet for all the attention the deal has attracted, the Lakers are not even the most valuable team in their league. For the fourth year in a row, that honor belongs to the Golden State Warriors, who _Forbes_ estimates could fetch $11 billion if owners Joe Lacob and Peter Guber were willing to part with them. No other NBA team was within $300 million of the Warriors\u2019 estimated $880 million in revenue last season, with the club far outpacing the rest of the league with its income from sponsorships and [premium seating](https://www.forbes.com/sites/brettknight/2024/11/24/how-modern-sports-arenas-make-millions-more-by-building-fewer-luxury-suites/ \"https://www.forbes.com/sites/brettknight/2024/11/24/how-modern-sports-arenas-make-millions-more-by-building-fewer-luxury-suites/\") and ranking in the top three in virtually every category tracked by _Forbes_. In fact, Golden State more than doubled the $417 million league average for total revenue, and compared with the other North American sports leagues, the Warriors surpassed every franchise except the NFL\u2019s [Dallas Cowboys](https://www.forbes.com/teams/dallas-cowboys/ \"https://www.forbes.com/teams/dallas-cowboys/\"), who topped $1.2 billion in 2024 revenue. Despite that financial muscle, not all NBA insiders agree that the Warriors should sit above the Lakers in the ranking of the league\u2019s most valuable teams\u2014or, for that matter, above the New York Knicks, who land at No. 3 on _Forbes\u2019_ 2025 list at $9.75 billion. Then again, not everyone believes the Lakers are actually worth the $10 billion that Walter, who is also the controlling owner of MLB\u2019s Los Angeles Dodgers, was willing to pay. Some bankers, for instance, suggest the club\u2019s true value might be better captured with a blended valuation\u2014perhaps between $8 billion and $9 billion\u2014that reflects both this year\u2019s purchase and Walter\u2019s initial 2021 deal for a minority stake in the franchise. The relative muddle at the top of the hierarchy stems in part from the fact that the Lakers\u2019 and the Kni", "relevance_score": 0.85}]
